researchResults.title: Bear Robotics acquires Kinisi Robotics to boost its physical AI capabilities
researchResults.description:
Kinisi's KR1 humanoid robot, its Bristol-based engineering team, and its physical AI capabilities will be integrated into Bear Robotics.

The post Bear Robotics acquires Kinisi Robotics to boost its physical AI capabilities appeared first on The Robot Report.

researchResults.title: FORT and NVIDIA launch AI-driven Outside-In Safety blueprint
researchResults.description:
FORT Robotics and NVIDIA launch Outside-In Safety, using external sensors to improve autonomous robot productivity and worker safety.

The post FORT and NVIDIA launch AI-driven Outside-In Safety blueprint appeared first on The Robot Report.

researchResults.title: NVIDIA releases Halos, a full-stack safety system for robotics
researchResults.description:
NVIDIA said Halos connects AI compute, system software, sensor data, safety applications, and inspection for robotic systems.

The post NVIDIA releases Halos, a full-stack safety system for robotics appeared first on The Robot Report.

researchResults.title: How Intrinsic eliminates manual robot coding
researchResults.description:
Alphabet’s Intrinsic has debuted its AI-powered "Intelligence Cell" to simplify factory automation and eliminate complex robot programming.

The post How Intrinsic eliminates manual robot coding appeared first on The Robot Report.

researchResults.title: Eclipse Automation launches RealitySync simulation platform
researchResults.description:
Eclipse said its new RealitySync platform allows manufacturers to step into their future factory before it’s built.

The post Eclipse Automation launches RealitySync simulation platform appeared first on The Robot Report.

researchResults.title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal
researchResults.description: What does an AI company do after one of those not-acqui-hire deals? Groq raised money, is leaning into its neocloud business, and is hiring new execs.

researchResults.title: Nvidia wants to cut data center water use, but that’s not the same as fixing AI’s water problem
researchResults.description: Nvidia announced a new cooling system that cuts water use inside the data center. But it does nothing to address AI's biggest water use — fossil fuel power plants.

researchResults.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
researchResults.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

researchResults.title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open source bugs
researchResults.description: OpenAI is using AI to help the open source community better protect itself.

researchResults.title: The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
researchResults.description: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.
